skill.name: api-testing
skill.description: Guides creation of pytest, FastAPI TestClient, and Playwright tests for backend APIs and frontend user flows.
skill.summary: Create backend API and frontend E2E test patterns for FastAPI and React applications.
skill.author: Awais68

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found documentation examples and a local validation script rather than malicious behavior. The weak cryptography, reconnaissance, sensitive file, and backtick findings are false positives caused by markdown fences, test data, localhost URLs, and benign Python regex/file checks. Low residual risk remains because users may copy test commands, CI snippets, and environment-dependent examples into real projects.

low_findings.title: External Command Examples Require User Review
low_findings.description: The markdown includes npm, pytest, Playwright, pip, and CI run command examples. These are not executed by the skill itself, but copied commands should be reviewed before use in a real project.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly shown as documentation examples, not executed by a bundled script. Low risk remains because users may copy them into their environment.

low_findings.title: Localhost Network URLs Are Benign Test Targets
low_findings.description: The detected URLs point to localhost Playwright configuration. There is no evidence of external network exfiltration or suspicious remote endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs are explicit localhost test server addresses inside Playwright configuration. No credential transfer or external host is present.

low_findings.title: Environment Access Limited To CI Configuration
low_findings.description: The process.env references read only the CI flag to adjust Playwright behavior. The database string is an in-memory SQLite test URL, not a production secret.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines reference CI and sqlite:///:memory: only. I did not find evidence of secret environment variables being read or transmitted.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are String-Match False Positives
low_findings.description: The high weak-crypto alerts map to regex parsing, dependency override cleanup, sample data text, browser names, and integration names. No MD5, SHA1, DES, or unsafe crypto operation is implemented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported lines do not perform cryptographic hashing or encryption. The only password hashing example uses bcrypt at SKILL.md line 112.

low_findings.title: Local Filesystem Checks Are Verification Logic
low_findings.description: The verify script reads SKILL.md and checks for scripts/verify.py under the selected skill path. This is expected local validation, not system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script only checks local files needed to validate the skill package. It does not enumerate sensitive directories or collect host information.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: Review did not find text that claims system authority, asks the evaluator to ignore instructions, or attempts to override the audit process.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted searches and manual review did not reveal prompt injection phrases. The opening section describes the testing skill normally.

content.value_statement: API and frontend test suites often miss authentication, validation, and data isolation issues. This skill provides practical pytest, FastAPI TestClient, and Playwright patterns for repeatable coverage.
actual_capabilities: Designs pytest fixtures for isolated FastAPI test databases.
Shows TestClient patterns for authenticated CRUD endpoint tests.
Provides unit, integration, and E2E test structure examples.
Demonstrates Playwright configuration for frontend user flows.
Includes CI workflow examples for backend and frontend tests.
limitations: Does not execute tests or inspect live application behavior by itself.
Examples assume FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, pytest, and Playwright conventions.
Project-specific imports, routes, and selectors must be adapted.
Security-sensitive test data still needs local review before use.

use_cases.title: Add FastAPI Integration Tests
use_cases.description: Create endpoint tests with a temporary database, authentication headers, and status-code assertions.

use_cases.title: Cover Frontend User Journeys
use_cases.description: Build Playwright tests for login, form submission, filtering, and details pages.

use_cases.title: Standardize CI Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Organize commands and workflow steps for backend, frontend, and coverage reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic API Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-testing skill to outline pytest tests for my FastAPI endpoint. Include success, unauthorized, validation, and not-found cases.

prompt_templates.title: Design Test Fixtures
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-testing skill to design pytest fixtures for FastAPI TestClient, an isolated test database, a test user, and auth headers.

prompt_templates.title: Plan E2E Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-testing skill to plan Playwright tests for login, create, filter, view, and validation flows in my React app.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Test Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-testing skill to review my API and E2E test strategy. Identify missing edge cases, fixture risks, CI gaps, and coverage priorities.

output_examples.input: Create tests for a protected student creation endpoint.
output_examples.output: A focused test plan covering authenticated success, missing token rejection, invalid email validation, required field errors, and database cleanup.

output_examples.input: Add Playwright coverage for a student dashboard.
output_examples.output: A browser test outline for login, navigation, student creation, validation messages, grade filtering, and detail page checks.
A checklist covering dependency installation, unit tests, integration tests, frontend tests, coverage reporting, and CI-only retry behavior.

best_practices: Use isolated test databases and reset state between tests.
Cover success paths, authorization failures, validation errors, and missing records.
Keep E2E tests focused on critical user journeys and stable selectors.
anti_patterns: Do not run tests against production databases or real user accounts.
Do not reuse mutable fixtures that make tests depend on execution order.
Do not assert only status codes when response shape and side effects matter.

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on FastAPI, pytest, SQLAlchemy-style fixtures, React, and Playwright.

faq.question: Can it generate complete test files?
faq.answer: It can guide complete test file structure, but imports and routes must match your project.

faq.question: Does it run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns and commands that you run in your own environment.

faq.question: Is it useful for frontend testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Playwright examples for login, forms, filters, and page objects.

faq.question: How should I handle test credentials?
faq.answer: Use fake accounts and local fixtures. Never use production secrets in test examples.

faq.question: Does it replace security testing?
faq.answer: No. It supports functional and integration coverage, but security review still needs dedicated checks.
